text.title: Utility Tree and ATAM
text.summary: Utility tree structure, (H,H) priority notation, slimmed ATAM process, and ATAM outputs.

rule.statement: Cap the utility tree at 7 quality attributes maximum, 3 scenarios per attribute as active drivers. Everything below that threshold is "tracked, not driving" — do not let it influence architecture.
rule.rationale: Uncapped trees produce 30+ (H,H) drivers, making prioritization meaningless. The value of the tree is the forced ranking, not comprehensiveness.

rule.statement: Priority notation (Importance, Difficulty): H=High, M=Medium, L=Low. Only (H,H) scenarios drive architectural decisions. (H,L) scenarios are important but technically easy — implement them without architectural investment.
rule.rationale: (H,H) scenarios are the architectural risk areas: if you do not design for them explicitly, they will fail in production. (H,L) scenarios are handled by standard patterns.

rule.statement: At most 3 (H,H) drivers per utility tree. If everything is (H,H), force a stakeholder prioritization session — it means the team has not made trade-off decisions yet.
rule.rationale: When everything is critical, nothing is. The utility tree only provides value when it surfaces the true top-priority scenarios that justify architectural complexity.

example.code: UTILITY ├── Performance (H,H) ← architectural driver │ ├── Latency: p99 < 500ms under 1M concurrent users (H,H) │ └── Throughput: 50K TPS sustained (M,H) ├── Availability (H,H) ← architectural driver │ ├── Uptime: 99.99% per month (H,H) │ └── Recovery: RTO < 30s on AZ failure (M,H) ├── Security (H,M) │ ├── Auth: 0 unauthorized access (H,H) │ └── Encryption: all data at rest + in transit (M,M) └── Maintainability (M,H) └── Modifiability: new payment provider in < 1 sprint (M,H)

section.title: Slimmed ATAM
section.p: Full SEI ATAM is 2-3 days. Use this slimmed version: 2-hour workshop, utility tree, 5 critical scenarios, sensitivity points, trade-offs. Document outcome in 2 pages.

list.item: Step 3: Build utility tree, label (H/M/L, H/M/L) (30 min)
Step 4: Write 5 critical (H,H) scenarios in 6-part form (30 min)
Step 5: Identify sensitivity points and trade-offs per scenario (20 min)
Step 6: Document risks, non-risks, trade-off points (5 min)
